event: Announcement of name change from PCOS to PMOS
evidence_text: The Endocrine Society, a global network of people dealing with hormones, announced that PCOS (Polycystic Ovarian Syndrome) would henceforth be known as PMOS (Polyendocrine Metabolic Ovarian Syndrome).

event: Announcement of the renaming of PCOS to PMOS
evidence_text: Researchers announced on Tuesday (May 12, 2026) at a medical meeting.

event: Publication of research letter in JAMA Internal Medicine
evidence_text: Dr. Terhi Piltonen of the University of Oulu in Finland wrote in a research letter published on Monday (May 11, 2026) in JAMA Internal Medicine.

event: Renaming of PCOS to PMOS announced
evidence_text: PCOS has been renamed to PMOS to better reflect its complex nature.

event: Renaming of PCOS to PMOS announced
evidence_text: The Endocrine Society...has decided to rename Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS) as Polyendocrine Metabolic Ovarian Syndrome (PMOS).
The National Institute for Health Research (NIHR), Jodhpur, was established on 27 June 1984.
It was later renamed the National Institute for Implementation Research on Non-Communicable Diseases (NIIRNCD) on 7 December 2019.
subsequently redesignated as NIHR on 29 April 2026.
